                3
 The Best Way to Contact Us

   •   Questions for the lecture, tutorial, assignments:
       Moodle → Course Announcements and Forums → Learning forums → Course Q&A




   •   Questions about yourself: Equitable Learning Plan (ELP),
       and other personal questions. Email LIC
       (jason.wu5@unsw.edu.au)!



                                                                                 4
 About this course
• This course is designed to challenge you and motivate you to develop critical
  thinking through a problem-solving approach
• Students learn AI and ML analytical tools better when they are applied to real
  life examples
• Hands-on activities and case studies on how to apply ML algorithms to
  conduct business analytics
• The emphasis will be on understanding the basics of each ML technique
 • How to put ML algorithms into practice to solve business problems
 • What the results mean and what actionable insights you could derive from
   your analysis

                                                                               5
 Course Learning Outcomes
• Demonstrate understanding of fundamentals of AI and ML
• Apply ML techniques to solve business problems
• Develop ML models to make predictions
• Assess the accuracy of the performance of the ML models
• Derive actionable insights/recommendations from the key findings
• Consider the ethical implications when implementing ML models to conduct
  business analytics
• Develop teamwork collaboration and gain critical thinking skills

            Programming is NOT the focus of this course!

                                                                         6
 What to expect from this course (Theoretical knowledge)

           A deep understanding of:
           •   Various types of Machine Learning Problems



                 Classification       Regression           Clustering        Anomaly detection




                Data generation     Recommendation          Ranking


           •   Applicability of AI to a variety of business analytics problems

           •   Role of AI in supporting decisions making in a variety of industries

           •   Ethical Implications of AI-driven Business Analytics

                                                                                                 7
 What to expect from this course (Practical knowledge)
           •   Hands-on experience with industry-grade AI tools




           •   Hands-on experience with AI tools in Python/R

     Weekly course activities
•    Preparation Activities (60 min)
     Reading, watching videos, complete case study activities, configuring software needed for the lecture
     activities.

•    Seminar: 3 hrs
     Theory & Knowledge (60 mins)
     Hands-On Activity (20 mins)
•    Quiz (preparation and participation) + Break (20 mins)
•    Case Study (30 mins)
     Hands-On Deep Dive (50 mins)

•    Consultations: 1 hr per week
     Tuesday 4-5pm; On Teams (or by appointment: jason.wu5@unsw.edu.au).
•    Or Email the tutor to do the consultation if the question is from the tutorial sessions.




                                                                                                        11
 Weekly course activities
To fully benefit from this course, you are encouraged
•   Complete the preparation activities before your arrival to class for the Seminar
•   Engage during the lecture & tutorial, and ask questions
•   Practice extensively the analysis outside the formal seminars to solidify your learning


Hands-On Deep Dives will be used to reinforce materials covered in Lecture
•   Very practical and designed to develop your analytical skills using plenty of exercises
•   Each week you will be given hands-on activities on analytical tasks to complete
    before/during tutorial sessions.

                                                                                           14
 Evaluations (All Assignments will be out in Week 2)




                                                      15
 What is Artificial Intelligence?
         “Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the capability of a
         machine to imitate intelligent human behavior. This involves
         the development and application of algorithms and
         computational models that enable machines to perform
         tasks that would typically require human intelligence”

                            Russell, S., & Norvig, P. (2021). Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach, 4th Edition.



                       Artificial intelligence             Machine learning
                       ﻿   Neural networks                          Deep learning
                       ﻿
                                                                                                                         16
 Why AI-driven Business Analytics?




                                    17
 What is AI-driven Business Analytics?




                                        18
 Why AI-driven Business Analytics Matter?
Big data has created a pressing need for advanced tools and techniques to process, analyse
the vast amounts of data generated every day
    This is where Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) come into play!
• Data-driven AI-driven Business Analytics relies on analysing huge amounts of data.
  The more data available, the more effective they are at producing accurate results.
• Handling Complexity and Volume
   • It requires computer processing power to manage large datasets
• Insights AI and ML can be leveraged to automate data analysis,
    • Identify patterns- trends, derive valuable and actionable insights
• Predictive models create forecasts and predictions to identify potential risks/opportunities
    • Empower organisations plan for the future- make informed and timely decisions


                                                                                                 19
                                 Let’s travel back in time to
                               explore the early days of AI!




A Brief History of Artificial Intelligence




                                                               20
 The early days of AI
Good Old-Fashioned Artificial Intelligence
(GOFAI)
1950s The Turing Test - Alan Turing wrote a paper
      suggesting how to test a “thinking” machine
  and      Development of Good Old-Fashioned Artificial
1960s      Intelligence (GOFAI) including:
           • Symbolic AI (logic programming, production
             rules, semantic nets)
           • Approaches inspired by human or animal
             cognition or behaviour (Hodgkin-Huxley
             model of the brain as neurons forming an
             electrical network, with individual neurons
             firing in all-or-nothing (on/off) pulses)
           • Heuristics models (applied to search
             algorithms)

                                                                                          The Second AI Winter - due to

 Classification

Machine Learning method                      Business applications
Classify data into categories (or classes)   • Sentiment analysis (Positive/Neutral/Negative)

• 2 classes = Binary classification          • Document classification

• > 2 classes = Multinomial classification   • Spam filtering

                                             • Image classification

                                             • Customer purchase/churn prediction

                                             • Credit card fraud detection

                                             • Credit-worthiness assessment

 Ranking

Machine Learning method                     Business applications

 Anomaly Detection

Find the outliers (unexpected events/items)   • Fraud detection in financial transactions
